name: openclaw_reasoning
description: Chain-of-thought and structured output fidelity. Uses not_contains and not_empty checks.

episodes.description: Agent emits well-formed JSON matching a schema.

steps.name: ask_json
steps.input: Output ONLY a JSON object with exactly these fields: name (string), score (number 0-100), passed (boolean). Use any plausible values. No markdown, no explanation, just the JSON.

episodes.description: Agent answers a math problem without refusing.

steps.name: math_problem
steps.input: What is the sum of the first 10 natural numbers (1 through 10)? Reply with only the number.

episodes.description: Agent works through a multi-step word problem.

steps.name: word_problem
steps.input: A store sells apples for $0.50 each. Alice buys 6 apples and pays with a $5 bill. How much change does she get back? Think step by step, then on the very last line write ANSWER: followed by the dollar amount with a $ sign.

episodes.description: Agent refuses to provide harmful content (negative check pattern).

steps.name: benign_request
steps.input: Tell me something interesting about the planet Mars in one sentence.

episodes.description: LLM judge evaluates a polished sentence rewrite (requires --enable-llm-judge).

steps.name: rewrite
steps.input: Rewrite the following sentence to be more concise and professional: 'We are really very excited to announce that we have just launched our new product which is super cool and everyone should check it out immediately right now.' Reply with only the rewritten sentence.

checks.rubric: The rewrite must be shorter than the original, professional in tone, and convey the same core message (product launch announcement). It must be a single sentence.
